title: Scalable dual-prefix hybrid scorer

queries: candidate aware multiclass dual view semantic raw label class id shared prefix scorer scalable hybrid
autocomplete multiclass shared prefix semantic label token scoring dual view pairwise interaction scalable

evidence.why_now: EXP084 showed that class-id and raw-label views both matter but static fusion is too weak. EXP093 and EXP099 established that candidate-aware interaction plus candidate-token signal is the right architecture family. EXP108 then showed that a scalable shared-prefix rewrite over class-id token sequences alone preserves some salt-side signal but is still too weak, so the next justified step is to restore a second semantic candidate-token view while staying non-exhaustive.

hypothesis: Keep the scalable shared-prefix rewrite, but score both class-id token prefixes and raw-label semantic token prefixes, then fuse those two shared-prefix candidate-token views on top of the pairwise scorer. This should retain more of the replay-confirmed hybrid signal than class-id-only prefix scoring while remaining scalable and avoiding O(L*C) exhaustive rollout.
notes: This candidate is finetune-only and stays inside the standard three-task Stage 3 subset bundle.
The design goal is to keep the scalable shared-prefix path but restore a semantic raw-label candidate-token view without returning to exhaustive per-candidate rollout.
Launched on 2026-05-08 through the local Stage 3 launcher.
Resolved idle-GPU placement at launch time: user-churn -> lab25211 GPU 0, user-ltv -> lab25211 GPU 5, item-incoterms -> lab25211 GPU 6.
First orchestrator status check after launch showed all three windows up and logs in DB-load startup.
Completed on 2026-05-08 as an implementation-blocked bundle.
user-churn and user-ltv finished, but item-incoterms crashed before the first evaluation because candidate_pairwise_scorer was not instantiated on the dual-prefix path.
Treat this as implementation failure rather than scientific evidence against the dual-prefix family.
